key_points: Matter is anything that has mass and occupies space
Particles of matter are very small
Particles of matter have spaces between them
Particles of matter are continuously moving
Particles of matter attract each other
Matter exists in three common states: solid, liquid and gas
Change of state occurs due to change in temperature or pressure
examples: Air, water and soil are examples of matter
Ice is solid water, water is liquid and steam is gaseous form
Perfume smell spreading in a room shows diffusion of particles
Sugar dissolving in water shows particles of matter have space between them
formulas: Temperature in Kelvin = Temperature in Celsius + 273
Melting point: Temperature at which solid changes into liquid
Boiling point: Temperature at which liquid changes into gas
exam_tips: Remember the properties of particles of matter
Learn the characteristics of solids, liquids and gases
Understand change of state: melting, freezing, evaporation and condensation
Use Kelvin scale in scientific temperature calculations
Give daily life examples for states of matter in exams
